title: Special Topics
description: Topics of interest not covered in the regular offerings.

title: Legislative Internship Program
description: Students work fulltime for the Georgia General Assembly for elected officials or committees. Students prepare research papers analyzing their work experiences relative to theory from the social science or policy studies. Spring semester only.

title: Elementary Russian I
description: Introduction to listening, speaking, reading, and writing in Russian and to the culture of Russian-speaking regions.

title: Elementary Russian II
description: Continued listening, speaking, reading, and writing in Russian with further study of the culture of Russian-speaking regions.

title: Vampires and Memory of Stalinism in Post-Soviet Russia
description: Gothic aesthetics in post-Soviet Russian and U.S. vampire fiction and films.  Post-Soviet fiction and movies as a source for studying historical memory.  Conducted in English.

title: Intensive Elementary Russian II
description: Continues introduction to fundamental Russian grammar and development of four modalities -- speaking, reading, listening, and writing -- in intensive immersion environment in Russia.  Credit not allowed for both RUSS 1692 and RUSS 1002 .

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of current interest in Russian.

title: Intermediate Russian I
description: A rapid review of grammar with continued use of listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills in Russian, all with a cultural emphasis.

title: Intermediate Russian II
description: This course focuses on listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills in Russian in an introduction to literature and within a cultural context.

title: Russian Modernist and Dystopian Fiction
description: Examines Russian modernist fiction and modernist dystopias: philosophical, social and political. Discussion of historical and cultural context, aesthetics, structure and meaning. Taught in English.

title: Intensive Intermediate Russian I
description: Review and extension of basic grammar, intensive vocabulary- building, and development of idiom on the basis of conversation, reading, and writing activities (in Russia). Credit not allowed for both RUSS 2691 and RUSS 2001 .

title: Intensive Intermediate Russian II
description: Review and extension of basic grammar, intensive vocabulary- building, and development of idiom on the basis of conversation, reading, and writing activities (in Russia).  Credit not allowed for both RUSS 2692 and RUSS 2002 .

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of current interest in Russian.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of current interest in Russian.

title: Advanced Russian I
description: First half of advanced courses in Russian conversation and composition. Discussion of controversial issues with the goal of self-expression in coherent paragraphs. Advanced grammar topics.

title: Advanced Russian II
description: Second half of advanced courses in Russian conversation and composition. Discussion of controversial issues with the goal of self-expression in coherent paragraphs. Advanced grammar topics.

title: Russian for Heritage Speakers
description: Russian-language communication skills for heritage speakers. Focus on grammar and orthography, stylistics of adult/professional communication. Reading, discussion and writing on contemporary topics. Taught in Russian. Credit will not be awarded for both RUSS 3005 and RUSS 3001 .

title: The Russian Twentieth Century in Literature and Film
description: Russian twentieth-century literature and film. Discussion of historical and cultural context, aesthetics, structure and meaning. Reading and discussion in English.

title: Russian and American Mass Cultures
description: Trends, genres and youth movements existing in contemporary American and Russian mass culture area compared to distinguish important cultural differences and similarities. Conducted in English.

title: Intensive Advanced Russian
description: Intensive development of aural/oral communication skills, capitalizing on a rich linguistic environment. Review and expansion of grammar, practiced in context. Focus on description, narration and comparison.

title: Advanced Reading and Composition for Business, Science and Technology
description: Advanced reading and writing skills. Study of syntax enables more sophisticated paragraph structure. Readings include newspaper texts on current affairs, business, science and technology.

title: Contemporary Russia
description: General introduction to political, cultural, social, and historical background of Russian domestic and foreign politics.  Lectures in English.  Discussion section and research project in Russian.

title: Special Topics
description: Permits a group of students and a professor to pursue areas of the Russian language not extensively treated in other courses in the department.

title: Imperial Imagination Through Literature and Film
description: This course examines how Russian, Soviet, and post-Soviet culture constructed the representations of the parts of the former Russian/Soviet empire. Taught in Russian.

title: Nineteenth-Century Russian Writers
description: Trends and paradigms in Russian literature from Pushkin to Chekhov. Conducted in Russian.

title: Russian Culture through the Prism of Song
description: Russian songs as object of study and focal points for broader exploration of key issues in Russian and Soviet culture and history.  Conducted in Russian.

title: Russian Intercultural Capstone Seminar
description: Russia and the West: cultural models and political fault lines.  Historical debates that continue to divide and engage Russians.  Conducted in Russian.

title: Intensive Advanced Russian II
description: Russian LBAT summer program. Reading on and discussion of contemporary Russian and global issues. Advanced grammar and syntax. Stylistics of written discourses. Taught in Russian.

title: Intensive Advanced Russian III
description: Russian LBAT summer program. Reading on and discussion of contemporary Russian and global issues. Advanced grammar and syntax. Stylistics of written discourses. Taught in Russian.

title: Russian Internship
description: Professional experience with a business/organization in which students enhance their language skills and cultural knowledge in Russian in relation on the practical goals/objectives of the entity.

title: Undergraduate Research
description: Independent research conducted under the guidance of a faculty member.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of interest not covered in the regular course offerings.

title: Special Problems in Russian
description: Provides the special instruction required under special programs.

title: Special Problems in Russian
description: Provides the special instruction required under special programs.

title: Technology and Sustainable Community Development
description: This course will explore the role of technology in the development of sustainable communities, locally and internationally.

title: Foundations of Sustainable Systems
description: This course teaches decision-making where a systems focus on the environmental and social dimensions of a design, project, innovation or business model are explicitly considered.

title: Introduction to Sociology
description: A survey of the discipline of sociology. Topics will include sociological theory, methods and selected substantive area, including social structure and functions, analysis of social processes, the foundations of personality, and analysis of social organization.

title: Elementary Spanish I
description: Introduction to listening, speaking, reading, and writing in Spanish and to the culture of Spanish-speaking regions.

title: Elementary Spanish II
description: Continued listening, speaking, reading, and writing in Spanish with further study of the culture of Spanish-speaking regions.

title: Intermediate Spanish I
description: A rapid review of grammar with continued use of listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills in Spanish, all with a cultural emphasis.

title: Intermediate Spanish II
description: This course focuses on listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills in Spanish in an introduction to literature and within a cultural context.

title: A Practical Application of Spanish Grammar
description: A review of important structures of Spanish, presented in communicative contexts vital to the academic careers of students.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Introduction to Reading Hispanic Literature
description: This course bridges language study and the study of literature by helping students read, understand , and interpret literature as a system of communication in Spanish.

title: Spanish for Business I: Fundamentals
description: Introduction to business language in the Hispanic world. Development of linguistic abilities to this end, with emphasis on those cultural factors that lead to commercial success. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Medical Spanish
description: This course facilitates acquisition of a broad vocabulary and awareness of cultural customs and trends pertaining to the healthcare field.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Latin American Music
description: Students study the musical traditions of the Andes, Mexico and the Caribbean, focusing on the rhythms, instruments, artists, style and tendencies present. Taught in Spanish.

title: Spanish Conversation: Issues and Strategies I
description: Development of communicative ability and cross-cultural awareness through discussion of contemporary issues in the Hispanic world. Conducted in Spanish. No native speakers allowed.

title: Composition: Analysis and Development I
description: Writings from the Hispanic world used as a springboard for analysis and enrichment of self-expression and development of precision in written communication. Incorporates grammar review. Conducted in Spanish. No native speakers allowed.

title: Cultural History of Spain II: Nineteenth and Twentieth Century Spain
description: History of Spanish culture from 1800 to the present. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Conversation Practicum
description: This course combines current event discussion with ongoing conversation practice.  Students participate in in-class discussions plus weekly language exchanges with native Spaniards.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Globalization in Latin America
description: Case-studies examination of globalization in Latin America regarding politics and industry, migration, culture and communication, and health and the environment. Taught in Spanish.

title: Spain Today
description: This course introduces students to current issues in the culture and history of contemporary Spain. Articles, videos, and news clips are used as springboard for discussion. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Latin America Today
description: Selected journalistic and literary writings used as a springboard for discussion of social, economic, and political issues of contemporary Latin America. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Society in Hispanic Literature
description: Study of Hispanic society and political thought in selected literary works.  Conducted in Spanish.

title: Spanish in Society: Authentic Cultural Narratives
description: This course offers an interdisciplinary analysis of the language, culture, history and politics of select Spanish novels and/or television series. Taught in Spanish.

title: Identity in Hispanic American Literature
description: This course examines the construction of personal, cultural, national, political, and regional identity in Hispanic American literature over the past two centuries.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Satire & Media in the Americas
description: This course explores how new types pf satire challenge traditional media and impacts political communication in today's democratic societies in the Americas. Taught in Spanish.

title: Science Fiction in Latin America
description: This course is a survey of Latin America science-fiction. Taught in Spanish.

title: Issues of Sustainable Development in the Andean Region
description: Historical and social context of contemporary issues of cultural, economic and environmental sustainability in the Andean region.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Cultural Patrimony in Peru
description: Using literature, film and art, this course examines cultural patrimony in Peru and its impact on society, economy, environment and threats to its preservation.  Conducted in Spanish.

title: Culture and Commerce in the Andes
description: Examines the 'business of nature' in the extractive economies of the Andes and culture clashes of Western and indigenous perspectives of development. Taught in Spanish.

title: Commerce and Sustainable Communities
description: Study of the connectedness and community impact of contemporary issues of economic, sociocultural, and environmental sustainability in Mexico.  Conducted in Spanish.  Part of the Spanish intensive summer LBAT program.  Admission by application only.

title: Business and Culture in the Hispanic World
description: Study of cultural issues, tendencies, and traditional patterns of behavior in Spanish-speaking people as they relate to business practices.  Value systems and formal manifestations. Regional variations, including the U.S. Hispanic culture. Part of the Spanish intensive summer language program. Admission by application only.

title: Science and Technology in the Hispanic World
description: Study of business organizations and use of technology in the Spanish-speaking world. Specialized vocabularies of business, economics, statistics, and computer science. Geographical and anthropological background.

title: Business and Culture in the Hispanic World: Seminar Abroad
description: Field study of technology, economic trends, business firms, financial institutions, and cultural protocols in the Spanish-speaking area. Part of the Spanish intensive summer language program. Admission by application only.

title: Spanish for Health Care Professionals
description: Culture-specific conceptions of health, medical vocabulary, professional-patient protocols, professional-professional protocols, symptoms, treatments.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Health Care Industry in Spain
description: Study of culture-specific values in health systems in Spain/ Europe.  Visits to local sites. Taught in Spanish.

title: Introduction to Caribbean Culture
description: An introduction to the rich cultures of the Hispanic Caribbean islands (Cuba, Dominican Republic, Puerto Rico) and their diasporas in the US. Taught in Spanish.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of current interest in Spanish.

title: Spanish for Science and Technology I: Fundamentals
description: Introduction to scientific vocabulary and discourse in Spanish, by fields of interest. Study of expository texts, numerical expressions, and graphic aids. Development of some reading and translation strategies. Writing feature descriptions and article/report summaries in Spanish. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Spanish for Science and Technology II: Applications
description: Advanced analysis of scientific and technological discourse in Spanish.  Focus on reading strategies and oral discussion of topics such as use and transfer of technology and the acculturation issues that follow. Further development of comprehension, production, and translation strategies, with emphasis on professional communications and on writing feature descriptions, summaries, and abstracts. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Spanish for the Professions
description: This course develops communication skills for professional purposes with an emphasis on specialized vocabulary building and cultural nuances of the Spanish-speaking world. Taught in Spanish.

title: Learning in the Hispanic Community
description: This class combines classroom discussion with Hispanic community service projects to allow students to study Hispanic cultures and practice Spanish with native speakers.  Conducted in Spanish.

title: Spanish Service-Learning Abroad I
description: Students complete service projects in a Spanish-speaking country abroad and attend weekly class meetings that combine student presentations and documentaries with a guest-speaker series. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Spanish Service-Learning Abroad II
description: Students complete service projects in a Spanish-speaking country abroad and gain an in-country immersion opportunity that hones oral and aural skills and increases cultural competence through real-life interactions with the host community abroad. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Social, Cultural, and Linguistic Diversity in Spain
description: This course examines the social, cultural, and linguistic dimensions of sustainability and the concepts of diversity and equity in the Spanish context.  Taught in Spanish.

title: U.S. Spanish: Language and Cultures
description: Linguistic groups, changes, bilingualism, customs, traditions of US Hispanics. Course project on current migration, legal, financial, educational, health, media or political issues/advances. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Bilingualism in the Spanish Speaking World
description: This course introduces students to bilingualism at both individual and societal levels within the Hispanic communities in the US, Spain, and Latin America. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Spanish Applied Linguistics
description: Advanced linguistic analysis of the Spanish language, particularly as it contrasts with English. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Nation and Narration in Latin America
description: This course examines the connection between nation and narration in Latin American literature and film and explores the narrative construction of national identities. Taught in Spanish.

title: Food, Power, and Sustainability in Latin America
description: Food as gateway to region-by-region study of issues of sustainable development in Latin America.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Visual Culture and Social Agenda in Latin America
description: Historical and cultural analysis of the role of art, visual image and iconography as social commentator and public platform in Latin America.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Hispanic Community Internship
description: Students complete inernships with agencies, organizations, and businesses serving Atlanta Hispanics, using their Spanish language skills in a professional work environment. Conducted in Spanish.

title: Span Sociolinguistics
description: This course explores the relationships between the Spanish language and the Hispanic cultures and societies of the world. Taught in Spanish.

title: Ibero-American Cities
description: This course examines the representation and development of cities in the Hispanic World.  Students develop three urban projects based on their study.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Covering Arts & Latin(o) American Culture in the U.S.
description: This course explores contemporary media reporting on the LatinX culture in the US and Latin America.  Taught in Spanish and English.

title: Globalization and Migration
description: Print and filmic texts serve as gateway to context-specific issues of migration in the Spanish-speaking world from both sender- and receiver-country perspectives.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Latin American Documentaries
description: This course explores the documentary traditions of Chile, Argentina and Cuba to learn about the history of Latin American societies.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Media and Power in Latin America
description: This course explores the tensions between media and power in relation to the struggle for freedom of expression in Latin American countries.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Latin America through Film
description: Contemporary Latin American film serves as gateway to in-depth discussion of social, political, economic and cross-cultural perspectives.  Conducted in Spanish.

title: Hispanic Digital Cultures
description: Students explore recent developments in New Media and Digital Culture within the context of Latin American and Spanish cultures and societies.  Taught in Spanish.

title: Advanced Intercultural Seminar
description: Integrates cross-cultural research and reflection into discussion of current issues in the Hispanic world. Intended for students who have had some study abroad experience in a Spanish-speaking country.  Conducted in Spanish.

title: Intercultural Dialogue
description: Explores cultural identity in Latin America through live interviews, print, and film.  Topics will vary.  Taught in Spanish as part of the LBAT study abroad program.

title: Sustainability in Spain
description: This course examines issues of economic and environmental sustainability as well as the relationship between the economy and the environment in Spain.  Conducted in Spanish.

title: Spanish Internship
description: Professional experience with a business/organization in which students enhance their language skills and cultural knowledge in Spanish in relation to the practical goals/objectives of the entity.

title: Undergraduate Research
description: Independent research conducted under the guidance of a faculty member.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of interest not covered in the regular course offerings.

title: Heritage Swahili I
description: For heritage students in the U.S. who have never studied Swahili formally, but who speak some Swahili.

title: Introduction to Africa
description: This interdisciplinary course will use a variety of approaches to study significant aspects of contemporary Africa’s societies, cultures, and representations. Taught in English.

title: Viscous Fluid Flow
description: Fundamental conservation laws.  Laminar flows, wall-bound and free shear flows. Separation, heat transfer, and compressibility effects. Introduction to flow instability and transition to turbulence.

title: Turbulent Flows
description: Basic characteristics of turbulence.  Statistical methods. Reynolds averaging, kinetic energy budget, and scaling issues.  Homogeneity and isotrophy.  Free and wall bounded shear flows. Simulation and modeling.

title: Advanced Aerodynamics
description: Introduce concepts, derivation and application of aerodynamic fundamentals. Emphasis on advanced knowledge in analysis and design of fixed-wing, launch/atmospheric return vehicles, and rotating systems.

title: Unsteady Aerodynamics
description: Unsteady potential theory for various speed ranges. Calculation of steady and unsteady aerodynamic loads on airfoils and wings.  Vortex flows. Topics of current research interest.

title: Computational Fluid Dynamics
description: Finite-difference, finite volume methods for solution of Navier-Stokes and Euler equations. Classification of equations, stability, grids, boundary conditions, implicit and explicit methods, turbulence modeling.

title: High-temperature Gas Dynamics I
description: Defining equations for compressible flows, real gas properties and their effect on the behavior of equilibrium and non-equilibrium flows.

title: Aeroacoustics
description: Lighthill's theory of aerodynamic noise and extensions, flow/acoustic interactions, feedback phenomenon, supersonic jet noise, aeroacoustics of ducts, propeller noise, helicopter noise, sonic boom.

title: Rotary Wing Aerodynamics
description: Vortex wake modeling; analytical inflow theories; modern computational methods for rotary wing aerodynamic analysis; aerodynamic noise.

title: Dynamics of Turbulence
description: Fundamental physics of turbulent flows.  Vorticity dynamics, Kolmogorov similarity hypotheses and nonlinear interactions. Mixing and dispersion. Direct and large-eddy simulations, Reynolds stress modeling.  Advanced topics.

title: Advanced Structural Analysis I
description: Stability of elastic systems under quasi-static loads. Classical, kinetic, and potential energy approaches through rigid member models.  Buckling of elastic bars and frames. Energy methods.

title: Fundamentals of Solid Mechanics
description: Unified overview of fundamental aspects of solid mechanics, from nonlinear continuum mechanics to linear elasticity, including an introduction to energy methods and other special topics.

title: Fundamentals of Aerospace Structural Analysis
description: Overview and fundamentals of aerospace structural analysis, including virtual work and energy methods, buckling and advanced structural theories.

title: Structural Optimization
description: Mathematical methods of constrained optimization, sensitivity analysis, approximation concepts, decomposition techniques, shape optimization in the context of structural design.

title: Advanced Aeroelasticity I
description: Understanding and analysis of aeroelastic phenomena in fixed-wing aircraft, static aeroelasticity, dynamic aeroelasticity, and dynamic response and transient stresses in aircraft structures.

title: Advanced Dynamics I
description: Kinematics of particles and rigid bodies, angular velocity, inertia properties, holonomic and nonholonomic constraints, generalized forces.

title: Advanced Dynamics II
description: A continuation of AE 6210 .  Equations of motion, Newtonian frames, consistent linearization, energy and momentum integrals, collisions, mathematical representation of finite rotation.

title: Rotorcraft Structural Dynamics and Aeroelasticity
description: Elementary blade dynamics, flap-lag dynamics, ground resonance, structural dynamics of rotating beams, nonlinear elastic blade analysis, harmonic balance and trim, Floquet theory.

title: Structural Dynamics
description: Dynamic response of single-degree-of-freedom systems, Lagrange's equations; modal decoupling; vibration of Euler-Bernoulli and Timoshenko beams, membranes and plates.

title: Applied Nonlinear Dynamics
description: Nonlinear vibration methods through averaging and multiple scales, bifurcation, periodic and quasi-periodic systems, transition to chaos, characterization of chaotic vibrations, thermodynamics of chaos, chaos control.

title: Optimization for the Design of Engineered Systems
description: Introduction to optimization problem formulations for engineering design, algorithms for constrained nonlinear programming, multiobjective and multidisciplinary optimization, and robust design optimization.

title: Rotorcraft Design I
description: System approach to conceptual design of aerospace systems with emphasis on rotorcraft. Comprehensive methodologies for aerospace vehicle synthesis and sizing. Integration of technologies. Students cannot receive credit for both AE 6333 and AE 4343 .

title: Rotorcraft Design II
description: Students work together on this application to complete the preliminary design stage of a specific rotorcraft. Participants are exposed to disciplinary and interdisciplinary issues.

title: Aircraft Design I
description: Stochastic approach to conceptual design of aerospace systems with emphasis on aircraft and missiles. Comprehensive methodologies for aerospace vehicle synthesis and sizing. Integration of technologies.

title: Aircraft Design II
description: Students work together on this application to complete the preliminary design stage of a specific aircraft or missile. Participants are exposed to disciplinary and interdisciplinary issues.

title: Orbital Mechanics
description: First graduate-level astrodynamics class that includes two-body orbital mechanics, orbit determination, orbit prediction, orbital maneuvers, lunar and interplanetary trajectories, orbital rendzvous and space navigation.

title: Planetary Entry, Descent and Landing
description: Entry flight mechanics and dynamics, aerothermodynamics, thermal protection systems, aerodynamic decelerators, descent and landing.  Robotic and human exploration mission studies for aerobraking, planetary entry, aerocapture.

title: Spacecraft Attitude Estimation and Control
description: Attitude representations, dynamics, estimation, and control. Spacecraft attitude sensors and actuators. Attitude determination and control topics applied to spacecraft and space missions.

title: Satellite Orbit Determination
description: Satellite Orbit Determination is a graduate-level course designed to provide the theoretical and practical knowledge to estimate the position of a satellite from tracking measurements.

title: Air Breathing Propulsion System Design I
description: Air breathing propulsion design with emphasis on multidisciplinary design issues related to system integration, cycle selection, performance, cost, reliability, maintainability, etc.

title: Safety by Design
description: Autonomous situational flight model allows students to examine complex behaviors in the "pilot-vehicle-operational conditions" system.  Flight certification and airworthiness requirements are mapped into formal scenarios.

title: Aerospace Systems Engineering
description: Introduction to aerospace systems engineering. Systems engineering and quality engineering methods and tools. Top-down design decision support processes, computer integrated environments, Integrated Product/Process Development (IPPD).

title: Advanced Design Methods I
description: Introduction to modern probabilistic design methods and techniques. Design of experiments, Taguchi methods, response surface equations, robust design, risk and uncertainty, technology assessment and selection.

title: Applied Design Laboratory
description: Introduction to computing tools and processes used in subsequent applied design courses in graduate fixed wing, rotary wing, and space systems design tracks.

title: Introduction to System of Systems Engineering Principles
description: Introduce students to the development and analysis of complex System of Systems, and their practical application to formulate Grand Challenge projects.

title: System of Systems Engineering Applications
description: Application of developed System of Systems analysis frameworks for the performance of Grand Challenge projects.

title: Combustion Dynamics
description: Acoustic wave propagation in inhomogeneous flows, flame-acoustic wave interactions, and control of combustion-driven oscillations.

title: Turbulent Combustion
description: Fundamentals of interaction between flow turbulence and reactive scalars.  Theoretical, numerical, and experimental methods. Physics of premixed, non-premixed, and partially premixed turbulent combustion.

title: Rocket Propulsion
description: Analysis and design of rocket engines including liquid, solid, hybrid, and advanced propulsion systems.

title: Electric Propulsion
description: The course provides a solid background of the operating principles, performance characteristics, and design features of the state-of-the-art electric propulsion systems.

title: Helicopter Stability and Control
description: Helicopter general equations of motion, rotor forces and moments, helicopter stability and control characteristics, handling qualities, flight control system design.

title: Random Processes and Kalman Filtering
description: Probability and random variables and processes; correlation; shaping filters; simulation of sensor errors; Wiener filter; random vectors; covariance propagation; recursive least-squares; Kalman filter; extensions.

title: Optimal Guidance and Control
description: Euler-Lagrange formulation; Hamilton-Jacobi approach; Pontryagin's minimum principle; Systems with quadratic performance index; Second variation and neighboring extremals; Singular solutions; numerical solution techniques.

title: Mathematical Principles of Planning and Decision-Making for Autonomy
description:
The course will introduce the students to the mathematical tools and the theory 
for formulating and solving problems that involve high-level decision-making for controlling and planning of autonomous systems with an emphasis on aerospace applications.

title: Advanced Flight Dynamics
description: Reference frames and transformations, general equations of unsteady motion, application to fixed-wing, rotary-wing and space vehicles, stability characteristics, flight in turbulent atmosphere.

title: Multivariable Linear Systems and Control
description: Techniques for analysis and description of multivariable linear systems. Tools for advanced feedback control design for these systems, including computational packages. Credit will not be awarded for both AE 6530 and ECE 6550 or AE 6530 and ME 6401 .

title: Aerospace Robust Control I
description: Robustness issues in controller analysis and design. LQ analysis, H2 norm, LQR, LQG, uncertainty modeling, small gain theorem, H-infinity  performance, and the mixed-norm H2/H-infinity problem.

title: Cognitive Engineering
description: Cognitive engineering addresses a range of technologies and work environments that will support human cognitive performance, including information systems, decision support, automation, and intelligent systems.

title: Aerospace Nonlinear Control
description: Advanced treatment of nonlinear robust control. Lyapunov stability theory, absolute stability, dissipativity, feedback linearization, Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman theory, nonlinear H-infinity, backstepping control, and control Lyapunov functions.

title: Graduate Internship
description: Graduate Internship for which the student is paid.

title: Wind Engineering
description: An introductory course on wind energy and its potential; modeling and design of wind turbines; analysis of the economic benefits of wind turbine systems. Credit will not be awarded for both AE 6701 and ME 6701 .

title: Introduction to Mechatronics
description: Modeling and control of actuators and electro-mechanical systems. Performance and application of microprocessors and analog electronics to modern mechatronic systems.

title: Evaluation of Human Integrated Systems
description: Evaluation of human integrated systems including translating research questions into measurable objectives, overview of evaluation methods and data analysis techniques applicable to such systems.  Credit not allowed for both AE 6721 and ISYE 6231 .

title: Topics in Engineering Practice
description: Topics of current importance offered in collaboration with an approved partner of Georgia Tech's Distance Learning Program.

title: Acoustics I
description: Fundamental principles governing the generation, propagation, reflection, and transmission of sound waves in fluids.  Crosslisted with ME 6760 .

title: Acoustics II
description: Radiation and scattering of sound waves in fluids, duct acoustics, dissipation phenomena. Crosslisted with ME 6761 .

title: Applied Acoustics
description: Mufflers, resonators, acoustic materials, barriers, industrial noise, room acoustics, active noise control. Crosslisted with ME  6762.

title: Kinetics and Thermodynamics of Gases
description: Thermodynamics of nonreacting and reacting gas mixtures. Introductory quantum theory, statistical thermodynamics, and gas kinetic theory. Crosslisted with ME 6765 .

title: Combustion
description: Introductory chemical kinetics, detonations and deflagrations, laminar flame propagation in premixed gases, ignition and quenching, laminar diffusion flames and droplet burning, turbulent reacting flows. Crosslisted with ME 6766 .

title: Linear Elasticity
description: Governing equations of linear elasticity, plane elasticity, boundary value problems, airy stress function and complex variable methods, simple three-dimensional solutions. Crosslisted with ME 6769 .

title: Robotics Professional Preparation
description: Preparation for a professional career in Robotics. Local & visiting speakers. Program introduction. Technical resume preparation. GT Robotics labs & resources.

title: Robotics Professional Preparation 2
description: Preparation for a professional career in Robotics. Local & visiting speakers. Selecting and applying for Internship. Developing capstone project proposal. Public speaking practice and preparation.

title: Robotics Professional Preparation 3
description: Preparation for a professional career in Robotics. Local & visiting speakers. Interview preparation & career search. Technical report writing and presentation.

title: Fundamentals of Fracture Mechanics
description: Advanced study of failure of structural materials under load, mechanics of fracture, and microscopic and macroscopic aspects of the fracture of engineering materials. Crosslisted with CHE, CEE, ME, and MSE 7772 .

title: Fatigue of Materials and Structures
description: Mechanical and microstructural aspects of nucleation and growth of cracks under cyclic loading conditions, notch effects, cumulative damage, multiaxial loading, and fatigue crack propagation. Crosslisted with CEE, CHE, ME, and MSE 7774 .

title: Topics in Fracture and Fatigue of Metallic and Composite Structures
description: Brittle and ductile fracture criteria. Failrue prediction in composite structures. Free-edge and internal delamination. Anisotropic cracks. Fatigue behavior of composites and metal. New micromechanical models. Crosslisted with CHE, ME, and MSE 7775.

title: Introduction to Robotics Resesarch
description: Familiarizes students with the core areas of robotics; mechanics, control, perception, AI, and autonomy. Provides an introduction to the mathematical tools required in robotics research.

title: AE Graduate Seminar
description: Introduce AE graduate students to world-class aerospace researchers and topics, discuss and demonstrate basic graduate student resources and skills.

title: Robotics Capstone Project
description: Teams or individuals apply the knowledge and skills acquired throughout the MS program to a faculty supervised robotics project.

title: Robotics Research Foundation I
description: Multidisciplinary research course supervised by two robotics faculty from different schools participating in the robotics Ph.D. program.

title: Robotics Research Foundation II
description: Continuation of AE 8751 (Robotics Research Foundation I).

title: Special Topics
description: Special topics of current interest.

title: Special Topics
description: Special topics of current interest.

title: Special Topics
description: Special topics of current interest.

title: Special Topics
description: Special Topics in AE.

title: Teaching Assistantship
description: For graduate students holding graduate teaching assistantships.

title: Research Assistantship
description: For graduate students holding graduate research assistantships.

title: Systems Physiology I: Cellular Mechanisms of Plasticity
description: The course will focus on adaptations of skeletal, muscular, and neural systems at the cellular level.

title: Systems Physiology II: Physiology of Neuromotor Tissues
description: The course will focus on function and adaptations of skeletal, muscular, and neural systems. Interactions among the various systems and their plasticity will be emphasized.

title: Systems Physiology III: Integrated Systems and Adaptation
description: The course will focus on integrative mechanism impacting motor system performance. Interactions among the various systems and their plasticity will be emphasized.

title: Studies in Rehabilitation Research: Prosthetics and Orthotics
description: This course will provide students in the PhD Training Program in Prosthetics and Orthotics to study issues in Rehabilitation Medicine.

title: Biostatistics
description: Introductory statistical principles and methods of experimental design, sampling, power estimation, and hypothesis testing using ANOVA and regression.

title: Biomechanical Aspects of Human Motor Control
description: The course will examine selected motor control problems that the nervous system faces in the process of managing this mechanical complexity.

title: Locomotion Neuromechanics
description: This is a course that will introduce topics  on the biomechanical and neural aspects of the control of limbed locomotion and movement.

title: The Aging Movement Control System
description: The aim of this course is to review research literature dealing with the effects of advances in age on the CNS and motor performance.

title: Mechanics and Pathomechanics of Movement Control
description: This course is designed to understand the potential effects of selected disorders of the neuromuscular system on movement control.

title: Methods of Human Neuroimaging
description: The purpose of the course is to introduce various methods of functional neuroimaging in humans.

title: Ion Channels in Health and Disease
description: This course will examine the structure, function and regulation of ion channels from both excitable and non-excitable cells.

title: Movement Disorders
description: This course serves as an introduction to the clinical and research aspects of movement disorders.

title: Cellular Physiology and Adaptation
description: This course will focus on adaptations of skeletal, muscular and neural systems at the cellular level.

title: Neuromotor Physiology
description: The course will focus on function and adaptations of the skeletal, muscular and neural systems. Interactions among various systems and their plasticity will be emphasized.

title: Integrative Physiology
description: The course will focus on integrative mechanisms impacting motor system performance. Interactions among the various systems and their plasticity will be emphasized.

title: Human Neuroanatomy
description: The purpose of this course is to learn the anatomical makeup of the human nervous system.  In this course we will closely examine details of central and peripheral neuroanatomy with links to function.  As well, comparisons with non-human vertebrate neuroanatomy will be made.

title: Muscle Structure and Plasticity
description: Covers the biological processes underlying skeletal muscle structure and function, as well as rigorous mathematical models of those processes.

title: Lower Limb Orthotics I
description: This course is the first part of a two course series and sets the essential elements of theory, technical design and patient management.

title: Upper Limb Prosthetics
description: Clinical training for the practice of prosthetics emphasizing adult and pediatric upper limb prostheses.

title: Spinal Orthotics
description: Clinical training for the practice of orthotics emphasizing adult and pediatric spinal orthoses.

title: Upper Limb Orthotics
description: Clinical training for the practice of orthotics emphasizing adult and pediatric upper limb orthoses.

title: Transfemoral Prosthetics
description: Clinical training for the practice of prosthetics emphasizing adult and pediatric transfemoral (above knee) prostheses.

title: Assistive Technology
description: Theories and devices associated with assistive technology and mobility aids, emphasizing topics important to clinical practice in prosthetics and orthotics.

title: Clinical Practicum in Prosthetics and Orthotics
description: Clinical observation of the practice of prosthetics and orthotics and related medical disciplines.

title: Capstone in Physiology Independent Study
description:
With guidance from an Applied Physiology faculty member, students will apply knowledge gained in prior coursework to
high-level inquiry into a physiology area of interest.

title: Seminar
description: The purpose of this course is for students to learn the research process from the early stage of identifying a question through publication of work.

title: Research Seminar I
description: A forum for graduate students in prosthetics and orthotics to present topics related to their research interests.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of special interest not covered in the regular course offerings.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of special interest not covered in the regular course offerings.

title: Special Problems
description: Individual studies and/or experimental investigations of problems of current interest.

title: Research Assistantship
description: For graduate students holding research assistantships.

title: Fundamentals of Modern Systems Engineering
description: Explore a wide range of modern systems engineering principles and development methodologies.  Address requirements engineering, systems definition, design and analysis, implementation, operation, and technical management.

title: Systems Design and Analysis
description: Introduce emerging techniques for systems and systems-of-systems analysis including IPPD, DOE, Taguchi methods, response surface equations, multi-attribute decision making, and concept feasibility assessment.

title: Modeling and Simulation for Systems Engineers
description: Introduction to modeling and simulation for systems engineers.  Topics include problem formulation, conceptual modeling, simulation methodologies, verification and validation, DOE, simulation execution, and output analysis.

title: Leading Systems Engineering Teams
description: Systems engineering processes provide a model for successfully managing complex systems.  Learn to apply management and development techniques used for successful commercial and government programs.

title: Advanced Topics in Systems Engineering: Systems Modeling with SysML
description: This core elective introduces SymML as a system modeling and design tool, with example applications, guidelines for application, and student projects on implementations in practice.

title: Systems Engineering Laboratory
description: Application of working knowledge of systems engineering techniques applied to a "case study" in an applicable domain.

title: Vehicle Systems Analysis and Synthesis
description: Vehicle Preliminary Design involves design iteration with disciplinary physics-based methods and tools.  Includes geometry and databases from conceptual design, six DOF modeling, analysis, and synthesized baseline.

title: System of Systems and Architecture
description: Develop a broader understanding of the interdependencies and interoperability issues, interfaces, and processes for creating and defining Systems Architecture for complex systems.

title: Complex System Lifecycle and Integration
description: System preliminary design must address product and process design throughout system lifecycle from integration through manufacturing to phase-out along with interfaces to other emerging systems.

title: Complex System Design and Integration (Capstone)
description: Student teams apply methods and techniques taught throughout the program to conduct complex system Conceptual Design based on requirements provided in a Request for Proposal.

title: Sensor Systems Analysis and Synthesis
description: Defines, classifies, and examines technology used in stand-alone sensor systems.  Centers on system-engineering trades useful in designing systems of sensors and systems of systems.

title: Information Systems Analysis and Synthesis
description: Tools and approaches for analysis and synthesis of enterprise information systems.  Topics include user-centered requirement, scenario-based design, UML, network/communications, iterative prototyping, and enterprise support.

title: Analysis and Synthesis: Human Systems Integration
description: Human Systems Integration Analysis and Synthesis, a PMASE complex systems elective, ensures human-related technical issues are properly addressed during system definition, design, development, and implementation.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of current interest in Applied Systems Engineering.

title: Special Topics
description: Topics of current interest in Applied Systems Engineering.

title: REVIT
description: Visualizing a form enhances a designer’s ability to communicate ideas.  Course will demonstrate how REVIT can facilitate the Conception of a design from various points of genesis.

title: Art & Architecture in Greece
description: An intensive on-site investigation of the role that the arts and architecture have played in the development of classical Greek civilization.

title: Art & Architecture in Italy I
description: Investigations of the painting, sculpture, and architecture of the Classical, Early Christian, Byzantine, and Medieval periods in Italy with special emphasis on Rome.

title: Art & Architecture in Italy II
description: Investigations of the painting, sculpture, and architecture of the Renaissance and Baroque periods in Italy with special emphasis on the works of Rome.

title: Media + Modeling 1
description: Introductory approaches to two and three dimensional modeling and representation in architecture using both manual and digital media and techniques.

title: Structures 1
description: This course provides students with a basic knowledge of analysis and design of building structures and the ordering of structural systems to resist gravity and lateral loads.

title: Media + Modeling 2
description: Intermediate approaches to two dimensional modeling and representation in architecture using both manual and digital media techniques.

title: Architecture Core I Studio
description: Foundation studies in architectural design emphasizing enalytical and analogical generative strategies applied to studio problems that engage architectural representation, composition, and fabrication.  Credit not allowed for both ARCH 6024 and ARCH 4021.

title: Core 1 Studio
description: Foundation studies in architectural design emphasizing analytical and analogical generative strategies applied to studio problems that engage architectural representation, composition, and fabrication.

title: Core 2 Studio
description: Intermediate studies in architectural design emphasizing integrative design strategies that engage the programmatic, contextual, and constructed dimensions of architecture and its representations.

title: Core 3 Studio
description: Intermediate studies in architectural design emphasizing integrative design strategies that engage the programmatic, contextual, and constructed dimensions of architecture and its representations.

title: Advanced Architectural Studio 1
description: Architectural design studio exploring advanced issues in architecture from the perspectives of professional practice, sustainability, technology and urban design.

title: Advanced Architectural Studio 2
description: Architectural design studio exploring advanced issues in architecture from the perspectives of professional practice, sustainability, technology and urban design.

title: Advanced Architecture Studio 1
description: Architectural design studio exploring advanced issues in architecture from the perspectives of professional practice, sustainability, technology and urban design.

title: Advanced Studio 2
description: Architectural design studio exploring advanced issues in architecture from the perspectives of professional practice, sustainability, technology and urban design.

title: Design + Research Studio 1
description: Advanced architectural design emphasizing innovation through applied research. Emerging methods of design generation/evaluation. Changing topics: healthcare, fabrication, urbanism, ecology, building performance, cultural institutions.

title: Design + Research Studio 2
description: Advanced architectural design emphasizing innovation through applied research. Emerging methods of design generation/evaluation. Changing topics: healthcare, fabrication, urbanism, ecology, building performance, cultural institutions.

title: Advanced Architectural Design I
description: Architectural design studio exploring advanced issues in architecture from the perspectives of professional practice, sustainability, technology and urban design.

title: Architectural Design and Research Studio 2
description: Advanced architectural design emphasizing innovation through applied research.  Emerging methods of design generation/evaluation.  Changing topics: healthcare, fabrication, urbanism, ecology, building performance, cultural institutions.

title: Retrofitting Suburbia
description: Study of suburban redevelopment trends, projects, and policies that improve environmental, social, and economic sustainability, with an emphasis on urban design strategies.

title: Architectural History I - Antiquity through the 18th Century
description: Architectural history from aniquity through the 18th century emphasizing buildings in their cultural context as informed by social, technological, and constructive factors and theoretical positions.  Credit not allowed for both ARCH 6105 and ARCH 4105 or ARCH 2111 .

title: Architectural History II - 19th and 20th Century
description: Architectural history during the 19th and 20th centuries emphasizing buildings in their cultural context as informed by social, technological, and constructive factors and theoretical positions.  Credit not allowed for both ARCH 6106 and ARCH 4106 or ARCH 2112 .

title: Intro to Historic Preser
description: This course provides an overview of the history, philosophy, organization, current legislation, policies, and practice of historic preservation.

title: Architecture and Minimalism
description: This course examines the influence of "minimalism," the 1960s art phenomenon, upon architecture culture and production from 1968-present.

title: Introduction to Art and Architecture in Italy
description: Required preparation for the COA Summer Program in Italy.  Includes the fundamentals of art and architecture; basic Italian language skills; library research for required summer program projects.

title: Form and Narrative: Cross Media Analysis
description: This theory course will involve an extended comparison between architecture and related art forms, especially painting, film, and writing.

title: Architecture and Ideology
description: Architecture and politics in Italy, Germany, and the Soviet Union between the wars.

title: Museums: History, Theory, Design
description: Investigates museums as manifestations of the construction and content of knowledge, the public mission of cultural and scientific institutions and the framing of visitors experience.

title: Theories of Urban Design
description: Contemporary theories of urban design and their relationship to the contemporary city examined through architects' writings, urban projects, and interdisciplinary criticism.

title: History and Theory of the Modern City
description: An examination of the evolution of the modern city in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries with particular reference to architectural, city planning, and urban design theories.

title: Contemporary Architecture and Urbanism in Europe
description: A course on contemporary architecture and urban projects in Europe, including the architect's writings, published criticism, and analyses of the buildings and projects.

title: Race, Space, and Architecture in the United States
description: This course examines the relationship between architecture - as social practice and as a labor market - and race in the United States.

title: Architectonics
description: The study of architecture of form from both an historical and a mathematical perspective.  The formal theory is applied mathematics, group theory, combinatroics, as well as recent studies in the history of mathematics.

title: Green Construction
description: This course focuses on the means, methods, strategies, and technologies to improve the energy efficiency and performance of buildings, and to reduce the environmental impact of buildings.

title: Architecture and Ecology
description: Introduction to ecological design theory, research, and practice in architecture, including writings, criticism, and analyses of buildings and projects.

title: Analytical Investigations in Urban Design
description: Measures of urban and spatial form.  Analysis of street connectivity.  Models of space use and spatial congnition.  Comparison and evaluation of design alternatives.

title: Construction Technology and Design Integration I
description: Introduction to building anatomy, technical and expressive characteristics of materials and their organizational assembly.  Credit not allowed for both ARCH 6229 and ARCH 4219.

title: Building Physics Modeling
description: Survey of basic thermo-fluid energy and mass flows in buildings, the interrelations between these flows, physical system modeling, and implications for building performance goals.

title: Evidence-Based Design
description: Fundamental concepts, research methods and case studies of evidence-based design.

title: Building Structures I
description: Introduction to design and analysis of building structures and ordering of structural systems to resist gravity and lateral loads. Emphasis on wood structures.

title: Advanced Architecture, Culture and Behavior:  Theories, Models and Methods
description: Theories, models, methods and case studies linking architectural design to culture and behavior.

title: Healthcare Design of the Future
description: Introduction to research-based approaches to integrated healthcare design innovation.

title: Urban Design: Policy and Implementation
description: Introduction to urban design policy and practice across a range of scales including planning, architecture, landscape architecture, civil engineering, public policy and administration.  Credit not allowed for both ARCH 6303 and CP 6834 .

title: Ecological Practice: History, Polemics, and Poetics
description: An historically and culturally grounded examination of the ecological perspective. Critical and productive engagement with green guidelines, laws, products, design briefs, and procedures.

title: Traditions of Architectural Practice
description: Critical examination of architectural practice. Cultural derivation and technological transformation of various conventions of representation, construction, and design; speculations about future paradigms of architectural practice.

title: Practice of Architecture I
description: Architectural practice from historical, sociological, and ethical perspectives with focus on professional leadership, practice management, and entrepreneurship.

title: Practice of Architecture 2
description: Methods of architectural project delivery and project management.  Fundamentals of building economics.  Emergent models of research-driven architectural practice.

title: Theory of Architecture 2
description: Approaches to architectural form, style, and tectonics from aesthetic, social, and technological perspectives.  Instrumental and symbolic uses of architectural media in design and building production.

title: Collage Making
description: Concepts of Collage within art, architecture, and culture, manual and electronic approaches to two and three-dimensional collage-making.

title: Building Furniture/Furnishing Buildings
description: A course that focuses on schematic design and design development of furniture.  The emphasis of this course is on conceptual and material/structural clarity.

title: 3D Modeling in Architecture
description: Construction of 3D computer models of architectural structures.  Topics include: geometry creation, light and materials property, rendering, data exchange, and basic animation.

title: Urban Ecological Design
description: This course engages the contemporary issues of urban ecology and its articulation to design.  It explores relationship between urban forms and flows of ecology, energy, material, water and information.  Credit not allowed for both ARCH 6447 and CP 6836 .

title: Architecture Modeling & Media 3
description: Advanced approaches to two and three dimensional modeling and representation in architecture using both manual and digital media and techniques.

title: Building Information Modeling - Concepts and Applications
description: Survey of Building Information Modeling - its technologies exploration of new technologies to be applied and new procedures of project execution.

title: Construction Materials, Systems, and Fabrications
description: Physical and constructional properties of building materials.  Fabrication techniques for building components. Digital representations of materials, components, and fabrication processes.

title: Shape Grammars
description: Shape grammars are a powerful formal system for the generative description, interpretation and evaluation of designs.

title: Advanced Productions
description:
Advanced integration of digital design and production technologies through Mixed Reality (MR)  
 geared towards collaborative immersive spatial design and production systems.

title: Research Colloquium
description: Introduces students to the processes of developing a topic of inquiry and delivering projects in architectural design, and to prepare students for the development of a final MS research project to be delivered as a final deliverable of the MS program during the subsequent semester.

title: Building Systems & Data
description: A focus on the overlap between AEC (Architecture, Engineering, Construction) processes and the building models and data that support them.

title: Environmental Systems I
description: Basics of heat, light, and sound applied to buildings. Thermal loading, passive thermal control, thermal comfort, climate, passive solar strategies, light and daylighting, acoustics.

title: Urban Design Studio 1
description: Urban design studio problems focusing on analysis, design, and implementation strategies for contemporary urban problems.

title: Urban Design Studio 2
description: Advanced urban design problems emphasizing the application of contemporary urban design research and multidisciplinary collaboration into the design process.

title: Media + Modeling 3
description: Advanced approaches to two and three dimensional modeling and representation in architecture using both manual and digital media and techniques.

title: Urban Design Workshop
description: Advanced problems in urban design and development focusing on the Atlanta region.  Integration of urban design theory and methods, economic development, political registration, and communication.

title: Architecture Professional Practice Immersion
description: Immersion for first year Graduate students experiences of the Professional Practice of Architecture.

title: Integrated Building Systems II
description: Approaches building design and systems integration through the making of construction drawings and specifications of metal frame buildings, including structural steel calculations.

title: Integrated Building Systems 2
description: Approaches building design and systems integration through the making of construction drawings and specifications of metal frame buildings, including structural steel calculations.

title: Integrated Building Systems 3
description: Approaches building design and systems integration via the design and detailing of a concrete frame building, including engineering of the concrete frame and the mechanical systems.

title: History of Urban Form
description: History of the city as a collective work of architecture with an emphasis on the city's physical form and space.

title: Foundations of Architectural Theory
description: Reviews thought and attitudes guiding architecture since the modern movement, their philosophical sources, and their relationship with architectural production. Defines architectural theory.

title: Design & Climate Change
description: This course positions design as an essential component of research that explores the sociocultural and eco-political dimensions of climate change as it applies to the built environment.

title: Cognitive Function of Visual Design in Architecture
description: Presents fundamentals of imaginative and cognitive functioning of visual design in buildings, and develops inter-disciplinary approaches to investigating visual form of buildings.

title: Theories of Inquiry
description: Introduction to research paradigms and their assumptions. The formulation of questions and frameworks of description, representation, analysis, interpretation, and data control.

title: Introduction to Architectural Research 1
description: Fundamental issues and methods across specializations in architectural research modules on causation and simulation.

title: Introduction to Architectural Research 2
description: Fundamental issues and methods across specializations in architectural research; modules on representation and interpretation.

title: Introduction to Architectural Research 3
description: Fundamental issues and methods across specializations in architectural research; modules on historiography and epistemology and on theories of design.

title: Architecture Space & Culture
description: Accounts of the social functions of architectural space and associated design choices, across a variety of building types and scales of environmental design.

title: Qualifying Paper
description: Preparation for Qualifying Paper in Doctorial Program in Architecture.

title: Special Topics: History, Theory, and Criticism
Special Topics: Professional and Social Practice
Special Problems: History, Theory, and Criticism
Prep Doctoral Dissertation
description: Preparation for Qualifying Paper in Doctorial Program in Architecture.

title: Teaching Assistantship
description: For graduate students holding graduate teaching assistantships.

title: Research Assistantship
description: For gratuate students holding graduate research assistantships.

title: Genomics and Applied Bioinformatics
description: Retrieval and analysis of biological sequence, gene expression, and proteomics data from public databases and other sources; applying standard bioinformatics tools to investigate biological questions.  Credit not allowed for both BIOL 6150 and BIOL 4150.

title: Biological Oceanography
description: An introduction to the major biological processes in the ocean, including primary production, elemental cycling, food webs, and fisheries.

title: Microbial Ecology
description: Advanced studies of microbial ecosystems, the specific roles of bacteria in maintaining ecological balance, and the evolution of the ecosystem in response to changing environments.

title: Marine Ecology
description: An overview of the ecological and evolutionary patterns, processes, and mechanisms affecting the organization, structure, and function of a broad variety of marine communities.  Credit not allowed for both BIOL 6417 and BIOL 4417.

title: Microbial Physiology
description: Study of the physiology of growth and metabolic activities of microorganisms.

title: Population Dynamics
description: Students will examine the ecological factors that affect dynamics, regulation, and evolution of natural populations, emphasizing the connections with mathematical models, genetics, and ecology. Credit will not be awarded for both BIOL 6428 and BIOL 4428.

title: Conservation Biology
description: This course explores the broad diversity of disciplines in conservation, with emphasis on biological phenomena. Analyzes contemporary issues affecting biodiversity, extinction risk, and policymaking.

title: Methods in Molecular Biophysics
description: An introduction to biophysical methods that are employed to study biological macromolecules and their interaction to gain understanding of how they function.  Credit not allowed for both BIOL 6478 and BIOL 4478.

title: Programming in Biological and Health Sciences
description: This course introduces students to the basics of coding, applied to fundamental biological and medical questions.

title: Immunology
description: A survey of modern immunology and its applications, with emphasis on immunological methods used in molecular and cell biological research.

title: Evolution
description: An introduction to evolutionary patterns and processes, including the history of life, phylogenetics, population genetics, quantitative genetics, molecular evolution, and other important topics in evolutionary biology.

title: Molecular Biology of Microbes: Disease, Nature, and Biotechnology
description: Molecular genetics of bacteria with an emphasis on experimental approaches, regulatory mechanisms in disease-causing and environmental bacteria, and biotechnology applications derived from microbes. Credit will not be awarded for both BIOL 6607 and BIOL 4607, BIOL 4608, or BIOL 6608.

title: Advanced Microbial Physiology
description: Advanced studies of selected aspects of the physiololgy of prokaryotic and eukaryotic microorganisms.

title: Aquatic Chemical Ecology
description: The course focuses on understanding the chemical mechanisms of aquatic signaling and the cascading effects on population regulation, community organization, and ecosystem function.  Credit not allowed for both BIOL 6620 and BIOL 4620.

title: Proteomics: Technologies & Applications
description: This workshop-based course explores the field of proteomics with a focus on the technologies used, their advantages/disadvantages, and their applications to real world problems.

title: Environmental Microbial Genomics
description: To introduce advanced concepts and principles of contemporary environmental microbiological research and assosciated bioinformatics techniques through representative examples from recent literature.

title: Foundations of Quantitative Biosciences
description: Introduction to quantitative methods and logic that enable key advances in understanding living systems, spanning molecules, cells, organisms, and biomes.

title: Discovery of Signaling Molecules
description: The diversity of chemical signals between organisms and their structural specificities will be presented along with chemical and biological methods for isolating signaling molecules.  Crosslisted with CEE 6756 and CHEM 6756 .

title: Geomicrobiology
description: Interactions between microorganisms and the geosphere, microbial energetics and genetics; geochemical controls on microbial diversity and activity.  Crosslisted with EAS 6765 .

title: Foundations in Molecular and Cell Biology
description: The goal of this course is to provide new students with fundamental knowledge in the general areas of prokaryotic and eukaryotic molecular biology, biochemistry, structural biology, and bioinformatics.

title: Advanced Cell Biology
description: Current topics in eukarytotic cell biology including membrane functions, intracellular sorting and compartmentalization, cell signaling, cell cycle, cytoskeleton, cell adhesion, motility, and current experimental approaches.

title: Cancer Biology and Technology
description: This course covers the major concepts of cancer biology as well as to state-of-the-art technologies that are being applied to cancer research, detection and treatment.  Credit not allowed for both BIOL 7015 and BIOL 4015.

title: Molecular Evolution
description: Evolutionary processes at the molecular level, organizations of genomes and genetic systems. Students will read and present up-to-date research articles in various topics in molecular evolution.

title: Programming for Bioinformatics
description: This active-learning, project-based course provides a rigorous introduction to scientific computing for bioinformatics, including Linux utilities, shell scripting and bioinformatics programming.

title: Computational Genomics
description: In this active learning class, students will learn to convert sequence information into knowledge through the use of computational genomics tools, applications and databases.

title: Advances in Bacteriology
description: Topics of current interest in the physiology and ecology of bacteria and applications to practical problems.

title: Advances in Ecology
description: Topics of current interest in the general areas of population growth and limitation, and the structure and stability of ecosystems.

title: Advances in Environmental Biology
description: Topics of current interest in environmental biology.

title: Advances in Molecular Biology
description: Topics of current interest in molecular biology.

title: Advances in Genetics
description: Topics of current interest in genetics.

title: Seminar
description: Weekly seminars on current research presented by various scientists in the field of biology.

title: Seminar
description: Weekly seminars on current research presented by various scientists in the field of biology.

title: Professional development in quantitative biosciences
description: Enhance the professional development of Quantitative Biosciences PhD students in preparing and submitting fellowships and in organizing scientific workshops and events.

title: Seminar in Quantitative Biosciences
description: This course will introduce QBioS to QBioS Program Faculty, allowing them to learn about QBioS faculty members’ research and academic history.

title: Current Research in the Quantitative Biosciences
description: The overall objective of the course is to enable QBioS PhD students to attend seminars on advances across all areas of the life sciences, including those by 4th year QBioS students.

title: Tools of Science Seminar
description: This course addresses issues important to all successful scientists and engineers such as: research ethics; collaborations between industry, academics, and government; women and minorities in science; balancing research, teaching and service; writing, editing, and reviewing, presentations; job interviews; time management; speaking to the public and media; and scientific and university politics.

title: Epigenetics
description: This course will introduce the basic concepts and mechanisms in epigenetics, covering topics ranging from stem cell reprogramming, organismal development, social behaviors, to human diseases.

title: Community Ecology
description: An advanced ecology course that covers classic and contemporary concepts, patterns, and processes in the field of community ecology.

title: Human Evolutionary Genomics
description: An advanced course where students will discuss primary literature and use computational tools to investigate how evolution has shaped global patterns of human genetic variation.

title: Origin of complex life: from cells to societies
description: This course examines the evolutionary origins of complex life. We will examine the history of life on Earth and evolutionary process through which complexity arises.

title: RNA Biology and Bitechnology
description: The purpose of this course is to introduce students (graduate and upper level undergraduate) to the fundamental concepts of RNA biology and to state-of-the-art biotechnologies that use RNA for medical and industrial applications.

title: Chromosome Biology and Human Diseases
description: This course explores the fundamental mechanisms governing metabolism of eukaryotic chromosomes and human diseases resulting from malfunctioning of chromosomal maintenance.

title: Microbial Symbiosis & Microbiomes
description: This course explores how symbiotic interactions with microbes affect the biology of other organisms, focusing extensively on the beneficial microbes native to the human body.
